title: Acaena pusilla
taxonomy: Acaena pusilla is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a small, shrub-like perennial native to New Zealand. The classification of Acaena pusilla places it within the genus Acaena, which includes a number of other species of caterpillar plant. The exact classification of Acaena pusilla is Acaena pusilla, a member of the family Rosaceae, subfamily Rosoideae, and tribe Aphanesae. It is further classified as a species of the genus Acaena in the family Rosaceae.
uses: Acaena pusilla, a small shrub native to New Zealand, has been used in traditional Maori medicine for various purposes. Its leaves, stems, and roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's medicinal uses include treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects. The plant's traditional uses are primarily based on anecdotal evidence and require further scientific study.

side_effects: Acaena pusilla, a small, thorny shrub native to New Zealand, is known to cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals due to its sharp thorns and sap.
